QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_3

question.text: Mark-to-market (MTM) margin in commodity futures is:

answer.text: Calculated daily as the difference between the entry/previous DSP and the current day's closing price
Paid only at the time of contract expiry
A one-time initial deposit when opening a position

explanation.text: **The Logic:** MTM is calculated daily by marking positions to the daily settlement price (DSP). The difference from entry price (or previous DSP) determines the daily gain/loss. **The Trap:** MTM is DAILY, not at expiry only. Initial margin is the opening deposit. MTM definitely applies to commodities. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers daily MTM for equity futures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** MTM pay-in/pay-out must be settled in cash before T+1 trading starts — failure to meet MTM obligations can lead to position liquidation by the broker.
